title: Wi-Fi Troubleshooting: Can't Connect to Corporate Network
text:
Q: What should I do if I can't connect to the corporate Wi-Fi network?
A: If you're having trouble connecting to the corporate Wi-Fi network, follow these steps: Windows: Step 1 - Ensure Wi-Fi is enabled on your device by checking the network icon in the system tray. Step 2 - Click on the Wi-Fi icon, select the corporate network, and click 'Connect'. Step 3 - If prompted, enter your network credentials (usually your corporate email and password). Step 4 - If you still can't connect, go to 'Settings' > 'Network & Internet' > 'Status' and click on 'Network Troubleshooter'. macOS: Step 1 - Click the Wi-Fi icon in the menu bar and ensure Wi-Fi is turned on. Step 2 - Select the corporate network from the dropdown list and enter your credentials if prompted. Step 3 - If the connection fails, go to 'System Preferences' > 'Network', select Wi-Fi, and click 'Advanced'. Step 4 - Remove the corporate network from the preferred networks list and try reconnecting. If issues persist, check for any network outages or contact IT support for further assistance.
